Furthermore, the city is about to
build fine new library building, which
will have to be lighted. 1t has fine
city hall, which has to be lighted; and
it has police station and a number
of fire stations that have to be lighted.


'Why should not the city have its
own power plant and supply its own
lights, both for streets and buildings


The article in the Press also disclosed
that some complaint has been made
regarding the quality of light furnish
ed the city. The company that has
the contract to do the lighting is relied
on solely to give the number of candle
power per light that is specified in the
contract.


The city has no wa~ OF testing the
flow of electricity to see that the
lights are kept up to the contract, and
never has tested the power of the
lights furnished.
